title: MCP tool audit prompt
description: Adds a prompt-type PreToolUse hook on every mcp__* tool call. Claude audits the tool via the Anthropic's /security-review skill, classifies it as safe or dangerous, refuses if it can bypass the local hardening, and remembers approvals so the same tool isn't audited twice. WARNING: this is a model-side check — Claude reviewing Claude's own intended actions — so it is non-deterministic and cannot be relied on as a 100% effective control. Use it as defense-in-depth alongside the deterministic file-access hook and sandbox, not as a substitute for them.

hooks.prompt:
You are about to call an MCP tool (its name starts with `mcp__`). Audit it before the call proceeds.

1. Identify the exact tool by its full name `mcp__<server>__<tool>`.

2. If you have already approved this exact tool earlier in this session, or have recorded an approval for it in your persistent memory, skip steps 3-4 and proceed with the call.

3. Otherwise, use the `/security-review` skill to review the tool. Classify it as DANGEROUS if it can do any of the following:
   - Read, write, move, or delete files outside the workspace root, bypassing the `.claude/hardening/` file-access policy.
   - Reach network destinations that `sandbox.network.deniedDomains: ["*"]` in `.claude/settings.json` would normally block.
   - Execute arbitrary code, shell commands, or other unsandboxed processes.
   - Read or transmit secrets, credentials, API tokens, or environment variables.

4. If the tool is DANGEROUS: refuse the call, tell the user exactly which capability triggered the classification, and ask whether to override before retrying.

5. If the tool is SAFE: record an approval for `mcp__<server>__<tool>` in your memory system (a short note keyed by the full tool name is sufficient), then proceed with the call. Future invocations of the same tool must short-circuit at step 2.
